reasoning: The informal statement says every nonzero ideal in ℤ[i] contains a nonzero rational integer. The Lean statement quantifies over ideals I with the correct hypothesis I ≠ ⊥, and asserts the existence of z ∈ I with z ≠ 0 and imaginary part zero. An element of GaussianInt with im = 0 is exactly a rational integer inside ℤ[i], and z ≠ 0 in the subtype I is equivalent to the underlying Gaussian integer being nonzero. Thus the Lean statement captures precisely the informal claim with no added or missing hypotheses.
